gaps: Submission API (U4/U5/BR/NRF filing payload schemas) is not publicly documented — not implemented, disclosed as a known gap.
Notification API (poll-based FINRA Rulebook / Draft Registration Filing notifications) has no confirmed response schema in public docs — not implemented.
FileX bulk file transfer has no documented REST surface — out of scope.

Confirmed via live production testing: registration/firm-family datasets (REGISTRATIONVALIDATIONINDIVIDUAL, INDIVIDUALDELTA, COMPOSITEINDIVIDUAL, FIRMPROFILE, FIRMREGISTRATIONSTATUSHISTORY, 4530FILINGS) require a FINRA credential entitled beyond the basic API tier. Affected commands (complaints new/list, registration individual/firm/timeline/validate-batch) return a clean FINRA 403 permission-denied error for basic-tier credentials; they are expected to work correctly once the credential is entitled for registration/firm data.

Confirmed via live production testing: FINRA public Query API has no per-CUSIP or per-bond TRACE trade-level dataset accessible to a basic-tier credential. trace search/trace liquidity were retargeted from a per-CUSIP design to FIXEDINCOMEMARKET/TRACEMONTHLYVOLUME (--sub-product, market-wide monthly aggregate). Per-bond TRACE report-card data (REPORTCARD group) is also entitlement-gated.

novel_features.name: Threshold-list escalation tracker
novel_features.description: See which symbols just crossed the Reg SHO 5-day threshold escalation point before it triggers a mandatory close-out.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a consecutive-day streak computed from locally synced daily Threshold List snapshots — FINRA's API returns only current-day data per call, with no streak concept.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this over a raw threshold-list pull when you need to know how many consecutive days a symbol has been flagged, not just whether it's flagged today.

novel_features.name: New-complaints-since-last-sync
novel_features.description: See 4530 customer complaint filings for a firm within a recent time window, without re-reading the full history.
novel_features.rationale: Filters live 4530 filing queries to a --since window computed client-side, narrowing a compliance check to recent activity in one command instead of manually re-scanning the full filing list.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for a quick recent-activity check; use complaints list for the full history or a custom date range.

novel_features.name: Fixed-income market-health snapshot
novel_features.description: One snapshot report joining TRACE, Corporate/Agency Debt Market Breadth, and Corporate/Agency Debt Market Sentiment for a time window.
novel_features.rationale: Fans out to four dataset families in one command and reports each dataset's record counts and raw records for the window; no single FINRA endpoint or existing wrapper joins these families together.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for a single fixed-income market-condition snapshot instead of four separate dataset pulls stitched together by hand.

novel_features.name: TRACE monthly volume trend
novel_features.description: Month-over-month trend in TRACE Monthly Volume trade count and volume for a product category (or market-wide if no category given).
novel_features.rationale: A basic-tier credential's only accessible TRACE dataset is a market-wide monthly aggregate with no CUSIP field — per-CUSIP trade data requires a higher entitlement tier — so the trend is computed per subProductCode instead, from data no wrapper otherwise trends over time.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for a month-over-month volume/trade-count trend for a product category; use 'trace search' for raw monthly aggregate records instead.

novel_features.name: Registration timeline
novel_features.description: Full chronological registration-status history for one person, joining Composite Individual, Firm Registration Status History, and Individual Delta records.
novel_features.rationale: Three-table local join — no live FINRA endpoint returns merged registration history for a CRD.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to see how a rep's registration status changed over time; use 'registration individual --crd' for just the current snapshot.

novel_features.name: Registration validation batch check
novel_features.description: Validate many CRDs from a file in one call instead of checking them one at a time.
novel_features.rationale: Reads CRDs from a local file and calls Registration Validation Individual once per CRD, turning N manual lookups into one batch command.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this before submitting a batch of registration filings to confirm every CRD is currently valid.

narrative.headline: Every FINRA Query API dataset, plus local trend detection and batch registration checks no other FINRA tool offers.
narrative.value_prop: FINRA CLI treats the entire ~35-dataset Query API catalog as a live-discoverable surface instead of a hand-curated list, then layers offline SQLite history on top so Reg SHO escalation streaks, TRACE liquidity trends, and 4530 complaint surges become one-command answers instead of manual CSV diffing.
narrative.auth_narrative: FINRA uses OAuth2 client_credentials: your API Client ID and Client Secret (from the FINRA API Console) are exchanged for a short-lived Bearer token. Set FINRA_CLIENT_ID and FINRA_CLIENT_SECRET, then run 'finra-pp-cli auth login' to fetch and cache a token — it refreshes automatically before expiry.

quickstart.comment: confirm the CLI is configured before touching the network
see every dataset FINRA currently exposes, discovered live
pull 30 days of Reg SHO short-volume history for a symbol
check whether a symbol is escalating toward mandatory close-out

troubleshoots.fix: Confirm FINRA_CLIENT_ID/FINRA_CLIENT_SECRET are set and run 'finra-pp-cli auth login' to refresh the cached token.

troubleshoots.symptom: Empty results from a dataset you know has data
troubleshoots.fix: Check the dataset's real group/name via 'finra-pp-cli catalog --group <group>'. Every confirmed group/name identifier is ALL UPPERCASE (e.g. OTCMARKET, FIXEDINCOMEMARKET, FIRM, REGISTRATION).
FINRA caps sync requests at 1,200/min/IP; the client retries with backoff automatically, but heavy scripted loops should add their own delay.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI for FINRA Query API data pulls (equity, fixed income, registration/licensing), compliance monitoring rituals (Reg SHO threshold tracking, 4530 complaint surges), and batch CRD validation before filing submissions.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI to submit U4/U5/BR registration filings — Submission API payload schemas are not publicly documented and are not implemented.
Do not use this CLI for FileX bulk file transfers — that is SFTP/HTTPS/S3 infrastructure outside this CLI's scope.
Do not use this CLI for real-time push notifications — FINRA's Notification API is poll-based, not a webhook system.

recipes.title: Track a symbol's Reg SHO escalation
recipes.explanation: Narrow the streak-tracker output to just the fields that matter for a compliance check.

recipes.title: Fixed-income market snapshot
recipes.explanation: One command replaces four separate dataset pulls (TRACE, breadth, sentiment) stitched together by hand.

recipes.title: Recent complaint filings for a firm
recipes.explanation: Filters 4530 filings to a recent window instead of scanning the full history.

recipes.title: Bulk-validate CRDs before a filing batch
recipes.explanation: Turns N one-at-a-time CRD lookups into a single batch call.

recipes.title: TRACE monthly volume trend for a product category
recipes.explanation: Pairs --json with --select to keep agent output small for a deeply nested trend response.
